name: Circular Chess
author: AGP
description: The medieval round game (Byzantine 'shatranj al-muddawara'), revived by Dave Reynolds of Lincoln, England, in 1983 and played for the World Championship in Lincoln since 1996. A standard chess army on an annular board of 4 concentric rings x 16 sectors (64 cells), topologically a 16x4 cylinder: rooks, bishops, queens and knights slide or leap around the rings (the sector axis wraps) while the ring axis is bounded. Each army straddles a line, the two lines on opposite sides; each side's two pawn groups march in opposite directions round the board to promote on the opponent's back-piece squares. No castling, no en passant, and a rook or queen may not run the full circle back to its own square. White (player 1) moves first.
